summary: Batch 5-6 Mobile Parity Sprint testing at 375px viewport. Tested Emergency WhatsApp integration, Care/Go/Play 3-tab+dimTab pages, and Learn/Paperwork dimension pills pages. Fixed 2 critical bugs: (1) EmergencyMobilePage.jsx missing useNavigate import; (2) emergency-whatsapp endpoint not registered due to route definition after app.include_router(api_router). All 18 features verified passing after fixes.

critical.issue: Endpoint at line 24156 in server.py was defined AFTER app.include_router(api_router) at line 21706. FastAPI copies routes at call time — routes added after include_router are NOT registered. FIXED: moved endpoint definition to before line 21706 (near other notification routes ~line 17475).

ui_bugs.issue: Missing `import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'` — useNavigate used at line 55 but not imported, would cause ReferenceError. FIXED: added import.

action_items: IMPORTANT: Review server.py for other @api_router routes defined AFTER app.include_router(api_router) at line 21706 — these are dead code and not registered. Lines 22940-21706 range has many @api_router decorators that may be dead code (e.g., @api_router.post('/admin/cleanup-product-images') at line 23864 returns 404). The duplicate emergency-whatsapp endpoint at line 24156 (original position) can be removed as the working copy is now at line ~17476.
Remove duplicate emergency-whatsapp endpoint definition at line 24156 of server.py (now dead code since working copy is before include_router)
NOTE: WhatsApp actual delivery depends on Gupshup credentials — endpoint returns success:true regardless but actual delivery is integration-dependent
critical_code_review_comments: CRITICAL ARCHITECTURAL BUG: server.py has ~2500+ lines of @api_router decorated routes defined AFTER app.include_router(api_router) at line 21706. In FastAPI 0.110.1, include_router copies routes at call time — any @api_router routes defined after this call are NOT registered in the app. This means routes like /admin/cleanup-product-images (line 23864), /admin/renewals/expiring (line 23598) etc. are dead code. Some appear to be duplicates of routes in separate router modules (analytics_routes.py, birthday_box_routes.py, membership_routes.py) which is why they seem to work — the working versions are in separate included routers.
EmergencyMobilePage.jsx was missing `import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'` — used useNavigate at line 55 without importing it. Fixed in this iteration.
server.py bottom of file (line 24220) has incomplete function `build_birthday_box` ending with `all_allergies` on a standalone line — this is a NameError at runtime if that function is called.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Batches 5-6 fully tested and verified at 375px. Both bugs fixed: (1) useNavigate import in EmergencyMobilePage.jsx; (2) emergency-whatsapp endpoint moved before include_router in server.py. All 18 features pass. Key architectural note: server.py routes defined after include_router(api_router) at line 21706 are dead code — the emergency-whatsapp endpoint was moved to ~line 17476. The original dead-code position at line 24156 still exists and can be removed. Test credentials: dipali@clubconcierge.in / test123. Routes to test: /emergency, /care, /go, /play, /learn, /paperwork — all serve mobile views at 375px.

emergency_whatsapp_404.root_cause: In FastAPI 0.110.1, app.include_router(router) copies routes from the router at call time (static snapshot). The emergency-whatsapp endpoint was decorated with @api_router.post() at line 24156, which is AFTER app.include_router(api_router) at line 21706. Python executes module code top-to-bottom, so when include_router ran, the emergency-whatsapp route hadn't been defined yet. The decorator at line 24156 added the route to api_router but NOT to the main app.
emergency_whatsapp_404.fix: Moved the endpoint definition from line 24156 to ~line 17476 (before line 21706). The route now appears in OpenAPI spec and returns 200.
emergency_whatsapp_404.reproduction: Call POST /api/notifications/emergency-whatsapp → was returning 404 Not Found

missing_useNavigate.issue: EmergencyMobilePage.jsx would crash with ReferenceError: useNavigate is not defined
missing_useNavigate.root_cause: Line 55 uses `const navigate = useNavigate()` but useNavigate was not imported from 'react-router-dom'. Other mobile pages (CareMobilePage, GoMobilePage, etc.) all import it correctly.
missing_useNavigate.fix: Added `import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'` on line 8 of EmergencyMobilePage.jsx
